default.hero: A year-by-year guide connecting courses, internships, and technical skill-building for your path in Computer Science.
default.highlight: This roadmap is a starting point — a guide to the classes, technical skills, and recruiting timelines worth thinking about as you navigate a career in software engineering, AI, systems, or data.

classes.popupText: Introductory computer science course covering programming fundamentals.
Course on how computers work at the hardware and systems level.
Core course covering algorithms and common data structures used in software engineering.
Course focused on analyzing datasets and building visualizations.
Course studying how programming languages are designed and implemented.
Upper-level theory class such as algorithms, complexity, or automata.
Advanced courses in areas like AI, machine learning, systems, or networks.

programs.text: Freshman and sophomore engineering exposure programs
programs.popupText: Early-career programs that introduce students to engineering roles and recruiting.
Traditional software engineering internships typically taken the summer before senior year.

programs.text: Faculty research and independent study opportunities
programs.popupText: Opportunities to work with professors on research projects or independent studies.

resources.popupText: Platform used to practice coding interview problems.
Curated roadmap and explanations for common coding interview problems.
Open-source guide for learning large-scale system design.
Research projects available through the Colby computer science department.

freshman.hero: Freshman year is about starting the CS sequence and learning the foundations of programming and computational thinking.
freshman.highlight: Best use of freshman year: complete CS15X early, build strong programming fundamentals, and start exploring different areas of computer science such as software engineering, AI, systems, and data.

classes.popupText: Introductory programming and computer science fundamentals course.

classes.text: MA 125 / MA 130 / MA 160 (math foundation depending on placement)
classes.popupText: Mathematics courses that build the quantitative foundation for computer science.

programs.popupText: Google’s freshman and sophomore internship focused on early software engineering development.
Microsoft program designed for early college students exploring software engineering.
Meta program for early undergraduate students interested in engineering careers.
Uber’s internship program aimed at developing early-career software engineers.
Jane Street program introducing students to trading and quantitative engineering.
Fellowship program connecting diverse students with tech internships.
Program helping students build practical software engineering skills.
Remote fellowship where students contribute to open source and collaborative coding projects.

resources.popupText: Focus on mastering one programming language well.

resources.text: Attend hackathons or CS club events
resources.popupText: Events where students collaborate on projects and meet other programmers.
Small personal coding projects to build experience.

resources.text: Focus on strong study habits early
resources.popupText: Developing consistent study and time management practices.

default.hero: A year-by-year guide connecting courses, opportunities, and practical next steps for your path in Economics.
default.highlight: This roadmap is a starting point — a guide to the classes, programs, and resources worth thinking about as you navigate a career in Economics.

classes.popupText: Introductory course studying how individuals and firms make economic decisions.
Introductory course examining national economies and economic policy.
Intermediate theory course expanding on microeconomic decision models.
Intermediate macroeconomic models covering growth, inflation, and policy.
Course focused on economic research methods and data analysis.
Advanced economics topics and capstone seminar work.

programs.popupText: Programs introducing early college students to finance, consulting, or economics careers.
Industry internships that often lead to full-time job offers.
University resources for networking, resume help, and recruiting guidance.

resources.popupText: Financial modeling training used for investment banking preparation.
Student resource and guide for breaking into finance careers.

resources.text: Mergers and Acquisitions class with Mike McHale
resources.popupText: Colby course focused on corporate mergers, acquisitions, and deal analysis.
